topics: Part A - List of Experiments Using Discrete Components (Any Six Experiments Mandatory)
RC Integrating and Differentiating Circuits - Transient Analysis with Different Inputs and Frequency Response
Diode Clipping and Clamping Circuits - Transient and Transfer Characteristics
CE Amplifier - Design for a Specific Voltage Gain and Plot Frequency Response Characteristics
CS MOSFET Amplifier - Design for a Specific Voltage Gain and Plot Frequency Response Characteristics
Cascaded Amplifier (CE-CE) - Design for a Specific Voltage Gain and Plot Frequency Response Characteristics
Cascode Amplifier - Design for a Specific Voltage Gain and Plot Frequency Response Characteristics
Feedback Amplifiers (Current Series and Voltage Series) - Design for a Specific Voltage Gain and Plot Frequency Response Characteristics
RC Oscillators - RC Phase Shift or Wien Bridge Oscillator
Transformerless Power Amplifiers - Class B and Class AB
Transistor Series Voltage Regulator - Design for a Specific Output Voltage with and without Short Circuit Protection (Plot Load and Line Regulation Characteristics)
Part B - Simulation Experiments (Any Six Experiments Mandatory) Using Open-Source Tools Such as QUCS, KiCad, LT SPICE, or Variants of SPICE Tools
RC Integrating and Differentiating Circuits - Transient Analysis with Different Inputs and Frequency Response
Diode Clipping and Clamping Circuits - Transient and Transfer Characteristics
CE Amplifier - Design for a Specific Voltage Gain and Plot Frequency Response Characteristics
CS MOSFET Amplifier - Design for a Specific Voltage Gain and Plot Frequency Response Characteristics
Cascaded Amplifier (CE-CE) - Design for a Specific Voltage Gain and Plot Frequency Response Characteristics
Cascode Amplifier - Design for a Specific Voltage Gain and Plot Frequency Response Characteristics
Feedback Amplifiers (Current Series and Voltage Series) - Design for a Specific Voltage Gain and Plot Frequency Response Characteristics
RC Oscillators - RC Phase Shift or Wien Bridge Oscillator
Transformerless Power Amplifiers - Class B and Class AB
Transistor Series Voltage Regulator - Design for a Specific Output Voltage with and without Short Circuit Protection (Plot Load and Line Regulation Characteristics)
Course Objectives: Familiarise the Students with the Analog Circuits Design Using Discrete Components
Familiarise the Students with Simulation of Basic Analog Circuits
Course Outcomes: Design and Demonstrate the Functioning of Basic Analog Circuits Using Discrete Components
Design and Simulate the Functioning of Basic Analog Circuits Using Simulation Tools
Conduct Troubleshooting of a Given Circuit and to Analyze It
Course Assessment Method: Continuous Internal Evaluation (CIE) - 50 Marks
End Semester Examination (ESE) - 50 Marks
Text Books: Electronic Devices and Circuits by David A Bell, Oxford University Press, 2008, 5th Edition
Electronic Circuits Analysis and Design 1 by D. Meganathan, Yes Dee Publishing, 2023, 1st Edition
